metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2955
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.4: "Cadherin-9 Mediates Trans-cellular Adhesion via Cadherins-6 and -10"
Cell aggregation assay:
-"To determine whether cadherin-9 can bind in trans to other cadherins expressed in the hippocampus, we conducted cell aggregation assays (Takeichi and Nakagawa, 2001)."..."we identified four heterophilic cadherin pairs; cadherins-6/9, -9/10, -10/6, and -8/11 (Figures S4C and S4D)."
- "We then expressed Cdh9-smFPFLAG in one set of CHO cells to simulate CA3 neurons and plated them with a second set of CHO cells expressing Cdh6-smFPHA, Cdh10-smFPMYC, or Cdh6-smFPHA and Cdh10-smFPMYC (Figure 4C) to simulate CA1 neurons. Immunostaining shows that cadherins localize at the interaction interfaces in all combinations tested (Figures 4C and S4E)."
- "All cadherins tested were enriched in the synaptosome fraction relative to lysates by P14 (Figures 4E). "
- "We co-cultured neurons expressing Cdh9-smFPFLAG with neurons expressing Cdh6-smFPHA, Cdh10-smFPMYC, or both Cdh6-smFPHA and Cdh10-smFPMYC and immunostained for epitope tags and the synaptic markers vGLUT1 and PSD95. We observed co-localization of every pairwise combination of cadherins at synapses (Figures 4G-4I). "..."all three cadherins simultaneously co-localize at synaptic sites marked by juxtaposed vGLUT and PSD95 puncta (Figure 4J)."

In Fig.2 and 5 the in situ consequence of the adhesion activity for the Cadherin-6/9/10 is shown via effects on spine morphology and LTP (not annotated).. Experimental description: - Overexpression constructs: Cdh6 (mouse), Cdh9 (presumed mouse), Cdh10 (mouse)
